proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a higher objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
Patients receiving treatment_sotorasib have a higher objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_sotorasib in the overall (unselected) cohort.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a higher objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib in the overall (unselected) cohort.
Patients receiving treatment_osimertinib have a higher objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_osimertinib in the overall (unselected) cohort.

analyses.code: import pandas as pd; from scipy import stats; tab=pd.crosstab(df['treatment_pembrolizumab'],df['objective_response']); stats.chi2_contingency(tab)
analyses.result_summary: objective_response rate 0.1739 on treatment_pembrolizumab (n=25008) vs 0.1640 off (n=24992); risk difference +0.0099, chi-square p=0.00341. Pembrolizumab is associated with a small but statistically significant increase in response.
Response 0.1695 on sotorasib (n=17544) vs 0.1687 off (n=32456); risk difference +0.0008, p=0.829. No overall main effect; consistent with sotorasib being effective only in the KRAS G12C subgroup.
Response 0.1699 on olaparib vs 0.1685 off; risk difference +0.0013, p=0.724. No overall main effect.
Response 0.1655 on osimertinib vs 0.1704 off; risk difference -0.0050, p=0.179. No overall main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients receiving treatment_sotorasib, those with kras_g12c=1 have a higher objective_response rate than those with kras_g12c=0 (i.e., a positive treatment_sotorasib x kras_g12c interaction on response).

Among patients receiving treatment_osimertinib, those with egfr_mutation=1 have a higher objective_response rate than those with egfr_mutation=0 (positive treatment_osimertinib x egfr_mutation interaction).

Among patients receiving treatment_olaparib, those with brca2_mutation=1 have a higher objective_response rate than those with brca2_mutation=0 (positive treatment_olaparib x brca2_mutation interaction).
Among patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab, those with tmb_high=1 have a higher objective_response rate than those with tmb_high=0 (positive treatment_pembrolizumab x tmb_high interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Among sotorasib-treated patients, response was 0.1745 in kras_g12c+ (n=2246) vs 0.1687 in kras_g12c- (n=15298); subgroup risk difference +0.0058, p=0.512. Logistic interaction logOR=+0.059, p=0.427. Hypothesis NOT supported: no biomarker-matched benefit.

Among osimertinib-treated, response was 0.1637 in egfr_mutation+ (n=1973) vs 0.1657 in egfr_mutation- (n=13003); subgroup rd=-0.0020, p=0.847. Logistic interaction logOR=+0.024, p=0.758. Hypothesis NOT supported.

Among olaparib-treated, response was 0.1463 in brca2_mutation+ (n=417) vs 0.1706 in brca2_mutation- (n=14582); subgroup rd=-0.0243, p=0.217. Interaction logOR=-0.177, p=0.275 (point estimate trends negative). Hypothesis NOT supported.

Among pembrolizumab-treated, response was 0.1967 in tmb_high+ (n=7111) vs 0.1648 in tmb_high- (n=17897); subgroup rd=+0.0320, p=2e-09. Logistic interaction logOR=+0.184, p=4.2e-04. Hypothesis SUPPORTED: TMB-high enhances pembro response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab, those with pdl1_tps >= 0.5 have a higher objective_response rate than those with pdl1_tps < 0.5.
Continuous pdl1_tps shows a positive interaction with treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response in the full cohort (i.e., the pembrolizumab effect is larger at higher PDL1).

analyses.result_summary: Among pembrolizumab-treated patients, response was 0.2093 with pdl1_tps>=0.5 (n=5342) vs 0.1642 with pdl1_tps<0.5 (n=19666); rd=+0.0450, chi-square p=1.6e-14. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

Logistic interaction pdl1_tps:treatment_pembrolizumab logOR=+0.591, p=1.6e-07. Pdl1 main effect non-significant in untreated (logOR=-0.137, p=0.09); pembrolizumab main effect at pdl1_tps=0 is logOR=-0.114 (p=0.007). Pembrolizumab response increases steeply with PDL1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response rate (worse performance status -> lower response).
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Logistic regression: ECOG 1 vs 0 logOR=-0.361 (p=9e-46); ECOG 2 vs 0 logOR=-0.765 (p=9e-77). Strong dose-response: worse PS markedly lowers response. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.
Per +1 g/dL albumin, logOR=+0.091, p=1.4e-04. Mean albumin 3.82 in responders vs 3.80 in non-responders (t-test p=1.4e-04). Hypothesis SUPPORTED.
Per +1 mg/L CRP, logOR=-0.0049, p=6.9e-04. Mean CRP 5.78 in responders vs 6.15 in non-responders. Hypothesis SUPPORTED (small but significant).
Per +1 NLR unit, logOR=-0.0050, p=0.384. Means 3.48 vs 3.50, t-test p=0.384. Hypothesis NOT supported.
Per +1 U/L LDH, logOR=+0.0001, p=0.51. Mean LDH 224.3 vs 223.6 (essentially equal). Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients on treatment_pembrolizumab, those with stk11_mutation=1 have a lower objective_response rate than those with stk11_mutation=0 (negative treatment_pembrolizumab x stk11_mutation interaction).

Among patients on treatment_pembrolizumab, those with keap1_mutation=1 have a lower objective_response rate than those with keap1_mutation=0 (negative treatment_pembrolizumab x keap1_mutation interaction).
Among patients on treatment_pembrolizumab, tp53_mutation status modifies objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Among pembro-treated, stk11_mutation+ response 0.1578 (n=3727) vs - 0.1767 (n=21281); rd=-0.0189, chi-square p=0.0053. Logistic interaction logOR=-0.211, p=0.0018. Hypothesis SUPPORTED: STK11 confers immunotherapy resistance.

Among pembro-treated, keap1_mutation+ rate 0.1712 vs - 0.1744; rd=-0.0033, p=0.625. Interaction logOR=-0.058, p=0.363. Direction consistent with hypothesized resistance but NOT statistically significant.
Among pembro-treated, tp53+ rate 0.1747 vs - 0.1732; rd=+0.0014, p=0.778. No effect-modification by TP53 detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with stage_iv=0.
Patients with has_brain_mets=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with has_brain_mets=0.
Patients with liver_mets=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with liver_mets=0.
bone_mets, adrenal_mets, pleural_effusion, pericardial_effusion, and contralateral_lung_mets are each associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: stage_iv=1: response 0.1540 (n=32365); =0: 0.1964 (n=17635); rd=-0.0425, chi-square p=1.1e-33. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.
has_brain_mets=1: response 0.1436 (n=12584); =0: 0.1775 (n=37416); rd=-0.0339, p=2.0e-18. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.
liver_mets=1: response 0.1728 (n=7546); =0: 0.1683 (n=42454); rd=+0.0046, p=0.34. Hypothesis NOT supported (point estimate is in the opposite direction and non-significant).

bone_mets rd=+0.0056 p=0.18; adrenal_mets rd=-0.0049 p=0.39; pleural_effusion rd=+0.0007 p=0.89; pericardial_effusion rd=+0.0046 p=0.61; contralateral_lung_mets rd=-0.0002 p=0.98. None reaches significance. Hypothesis NOT supported as a generalization beyond stage_iv and brain mets.

proposed_hypotheses.text: sex_female=1 patients have a different objective_response rate than sex_female=0 patients (test for sex disparity).
age_years is associated with objective_response rate.
objective_response rate differs by race_ethnicity (with white as reference).
objective_response rate differs by insurance_type (with private as reference); medicaid/uninsured patients have lower response.
rural_residence=1 is associated with lower objective_response.
More years of education_years is associated with higher objective_response.
smoking_status (never, former, current) is associated with objective_response.
treatment_pembrolizumab response is higher in current/former smokers than in never-smokers (positive pembro x smoking interaction relative to never).

analyses.result_summary: Female: 0.1754 (n=22371); male: 0.1637 (n=27629); rd=+0.0116, chi-square p=5.9e-04. Females have a small but significantly higher response rate.
Per +1 year, logOR=+0.0005, p=0.67. No association.

Response by race: white 0.165, black 0.178, hispanic 0.172, asian 0.177, other 0.185. Black vs white logOR=+0.085 p=0.021 (significant); asian vs white logOR=+0.083 p=0.099; hispanic vs white +0.043 p=0.21; other +0.138 p=0.094. Modest but mostly non-significant differences; if anything, white patients have the LOWEST rate.

Means: private 0.172, medicare 0.168, medicaid 0.161, uninsured 0.170. Medicaid vs private logOR=-0.077 p=0.040 (significant); other categories non-significant. Modest insurance disparity for medicaid only.
Rural 0.1666 (n=9910) vs non-rural 0.1695 (n=40090); rd=-0.0029, p=0.50. Hypothesis NOT supported.
Per +1 year education, logOR=-0.0034, p=0.40. Hypothesis NOT supported.
current vs former logOR=+0.027 p=0.32; never vs former logOR=-0.034 p=0.34. Means: current 0.172, former 0.168, never 0.164. No significant association.

Pembro x current (vs never): logOR=+0.078, p=0.31; pembro x former (vs never): logOR=+0.068, p=0.33. Direction is hypothesis-consistent (smokers respond better to pembro) but neither reaches significance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher fatigue_grade is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher pain_nrs is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher dyspnea_grade is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher cough_grade is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher appetite_loss_grade is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher bmi is associated with higher objective_response.
histology=='squamous' is associated with a different objective_response than 'adenocarcinoma'.

analyses.result_summary: Per +1% weight loss in last 6 mo: logOR=-0.0385, p=1.8e-32. Mean weight loss 3.4% in responders vs 3.9% in non-responders. Hypothesis STRONGLY SUPPORTED.
Per +1 grade fatigue, logOR=-0.022, p=0.040. Modestly supported.
Per +1 pain NRS, logOR=+0.0013, p=0.78. Hypothesis NOT supported.
Per +1 dyspnea grade, logOR=-0.0074, p=0.49. Direction consistent but NOT significant.
Per +1 cough grade, logOR=+0.0123, p=0.26. Direction opposite to hypothesis; not significant.
Per +1 appetite loss grade, logOR=-0.0104, p=0.35. Direction consistent but NOT significant.
Per +1 BMI unit, logOR=+0.0022, p=0.35. Direction consistent (higher BMI -> higher response) but NOT significant.
Squamous vs adenocarcinoma: logOR=+0.047, p=0.076. Adeno 0.167, squamous 0.174. Trend (squamous slightly higher) but NOT significant at 0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 23 individual snp_* variants (e.g. snp_rs1045642, snp_rs1065852, ... snp_rs1050828) shows a statistically significant univariate association with objective_response after Bonferroni correction.
The 23 SNPs jointly explain a non-trivial amount of variance in objective_response (likelihood-ratio test of full SNP model vs intercept-only is significant).

analyses.code: for s in snps: smf.logit(f'objective_response ~ {s}', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Of 23 SNPs, 2 reached uncorrected p<0.05 (snp_rs7412 logOR=+0.100 p=0.018; snp_rs1050828 logOR=-0.099 p=0.031), against 1.15 expected by chance. ZERO survive Bonferroni correction (threshold 0.0022). Hypothesis NOT supported.
Likelihood-ratio test of all 23 SNPs jointly: LR=19.39, df=23, p=0.68. Hypothesis NOT supported; SNP panel adds no meaningful predictive value.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for clinical covariates (ECOG, age, sex, stage_iv, brain mets, weight loss, albumin, CRP, histology, smoking, TMB, STK11), the positive treatment_pembrolizumab x pdl1_tps interaction on objective_response remains.
After multivariable adjustment, the positive treatment_pembrolizumab x tmb_high interaction on objective_response remains.
After multivariable adjustment, the negative treatment_pembrolizumab x stk11_mutation interaction on objective_response remains.
Independent prognostic factors for objective_response in a multivariable model are: ECOG, stage_iv, brain mets, weight loss (negative); albumin, sex_female (positive); CRP (negative).

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted treatment_pembrolizumab:pdl1_tps logOR=+0.596, p=1.6e-07. Predicted response on pembro: 0.155 at PDL1=0 vs 0.201 at PDL1=0.7 (off pembro: 0.169 -> 0.157). Hypothesis SUPPORTED.
Adjusted treatment_pembrolizumab:tmb_high logOR=+0.173, p=9.8e-04. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.
Adjusted treatment_pembrolizumab:stk11_mutation logOR=-0.204, p=0.0028. Hypothesis SUPPORTED: STK11 attenuates pembrolizumab benefit even after adjustment.

Adjusted independent predictors of objective_response: ECOG 1 vs 0 logOR=-0.368 (p=4e-47), ECOG 2 vs 0 -0.777 (p=2e-78); stage_iv -0.302 (p=1e-34); has_brain_mets -0.257 (p=8e-19); weight_loss_pct_6mo -0.039 (p=2e-33); albumin_g_dl +0.092 (p=1e-04); crp_mg_l -0.005 (p=4e-04); sex_female +0.081 (p=8e-04). Histology, smoking, age remained non-significant. Hypothesis SUPPORTED for all listed predictors.
